text.title: Staged-Only Scope as the Pre-Commit Rule
text.summary: Pre-commit hooks receive the staged file list — never the repository root — so the hook stays sub-second on every commit.

section.title: The staged-files contract
section.p: Each pre-commit framework has a built-in mechanism to pass the staged file set to the underlying tool. Use it. The whole-tree variants belong in CI, not in a hook.

rule.statement: Every linter or formatter invoked by a `pre-commit`, `lefthook`, or `husky+lint-staged` hook MUST receive ONLY the staged file paths. With `pre-commit` this means leaving `pass_filenames: true` (default) and never wrapping the command in a way that drops `$@`. With `lefthook`, every `run:` MUST reference `{staged_files}` (or `{push_files}` for pre-push). With husky, every command MUST go through `lint-staged` matching the relevant glob. Calling the lint or format tool with `.` or no argument from a pre-commit hook is forbidden.
rule.rationale: The PkgPulse 2026 husky-vs-lefthook benchmark, the lint-staged maintainers' own README, and pre-commit.com's `pass_filenames` docs all converge: whole-tree linting in a hook is the leading cause of `--no-verify` adoption. A 1500-file monorepo with `eslint .` per commit yields 8-12 second hooks; the same hook on three staged files runs in 200ms. Whole-tree formatters also rewrite unstaged work, polluting the next commit and breaking `git blame`.

example.note: Each tool sees only the staged files of its glob. Husky calls `npx lint-staged` and is done in 200ms.

example.code: # .husky/pre-commit npm run lint # which runs `eslint .` npm run format # which runs `prettier --write .`
example.note: Whole-tree on every commit. Each commit waits ~10 seconds and rewrites unstaged files.

antipattern.description: Wrapping a hook command in `bash -c "ruff check --fix ."` to "make it work the same in CI and locally."
antipattern.reason: The hook now ignores the staged file list because the wrapper drops `$@`. The CI/local parity is achieved instead by having a CI step that runs `ruff check .` on the whole tree separately — never by inflating the hook.

section.reference: pre-commit framework: filtering files by type / regex.
lint-staged: run linters on staged files.
PkgPulse 2026 — husky vs lefthook vs lint-staged benchmark.

section.title: CI runs the whole-tree gate
section.p: The whole-tree run still happens — but exactly once per PR, in CI, where its 12-second cost is amortized across reviewers.

rule.statement: The CI pipeline MUST include a separate job that runs each linter/formatter whole-tree (`eslint .`, `ruff check .`, `biome check .`, `mypy .`) against the merged-tree state of the PR. This job is the final pre-merge gate; it MUST be required in branch protection. The hook and the CI job share the same config but have opposite scopes by design.
rule.rationale: Hooks catch what the developer just touched; CI catches drift introduced by partial-stage workflows or by cross-file refactors that the hook's per-file scope cannot see. Both are needed; conflating them into one whole-tree hook breaks the local feedback loop.

example.code: # .github/workflows/ci.yml fragment - run: ruff check . - run: ruff format --check . - run: biome check .
example.note: Whole-tree commands live in CI, not in the local hook.
